DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT RUBIN GLICKMAN'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES, DEMAND FOR INSPECTION, & TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION; REQ. FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SHALL PROVIDE VERIFIED, CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION TO DEFENDANT RUBIN GLICKMAN'S FORM INTERROGATORIES - GENERAL, SET ONE AND DEFENDANT RUBIN GLICKMAN'S DEMAND FOR INSPECTION OF DOCUMENTS - SET ONE, AND PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO THE INSPECTION DEMAND WITHIN 15 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. PLAINTIFF SHALL FURTHER APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION WITHIN 15 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. PLAINTIFF SHALL PAY DEFENDANT THE SUM OF $375.00 WITHIN 15 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. PRO TEM JUDGE: ERNST HALPERIN CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

Mtn To Vacate Order Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JANE LUCIANO OF THE PHYSICIANS' ADVOCATES, TEL. NO. (510) 841-7500 SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. SPECIALLY APPEARING PLAINTIFF ANNA MEDEIROS' Motion To Vacate Order Terminating Sanctions pursuant to CCP SECTION 473 is granted, since no opposition was filed. The request for attorneys fees and costs made by specially appearing attorney for Plaintiff is denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

DEFENDANT RUBIN GLICKMAN'S Motion For Order Terminating Sanctions Or In Alternative For Further Order To Compelling Responses To Interrogatories, Demand For Inspection & To Appear For Deposition & Request For Monetary Sanctions - To be heard at 9:00 am on the Discovery calendar. Defendant Rubin Glickman'S Motion For Order Terminating Sanctions Or In Alternative For Further Order Compelling Responses To Interrogatories, Demand For Inspection & To Appear For Deposition & Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:

Defendant Rubin Glickman's Motion For Order Terminating Sanctions Or In Alternative For Further Order Compelling Responses To Interrogatories, Demand For Inspection & To Appear For Deposition & Request For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED as unopposed motion. James Watson, Judge Pro-Tem, Jacqueline Alameda, Clerk, Not Reported. =302/JPT

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Compel Responses To Interrogatories, Demand For Inspection, & To Appear For Deposition; Req. For Monetary Sanctions; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
Off Calendar Sep-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARK P. EDSON OF WINTERS & KRUG, TEL. NO. (650) 579-1422 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT RUBIN GLICKMAN'S motion for Order To Compel Responses To Interrogatories, Demand For Inspection, & To Appear For Deposition; Req. For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. Plaintiff shall provide verified, code compliant responses without objection to Defendant Rubin Glickman's Form Interrogatories - General, Set One and Defendant Rubin Glickman's Demand for Inspection of Documents - Set One, and produce all documents responsive to the inspection demand within 15 days of entry of this Order. Plaintiff shall further appear for deposition within 15 days of entry of this order. Plaintiff shall pay defendant the sum of $375.00 within 15 days of entry of this order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Pro Tem Judge: Ernst Halperin Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/jpt
